reforms.description: The government has set up a new National Productivity Investment Fund aimed at increasing public spending in critical areas for productivity, including research and development (R&D). This initiative is intended to enhance the UK's R&D spending, which is currently below the OECD average.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased public investment in R&D is expected to stimulate innovation and productivity growth across the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The Fund will allocate a budget of GBP 23 billion by 2021-22.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant increase in public investment in R&D, which is crucial for enhancing the UK's innovation capacity and addressing productivity challenges.
reforms.source_quote: The government has set up a new National Productivity Investment Fund with a view to increase public spending in areas that are critical for productivity: infrastructure, research and development (R&D) and housing.

reforms.description: The government plans to complement existing tax relief schemes by further increasing direct funding of R&D, which would enhance both the invention and diffusion of new technologies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing direct funding for R&D is expected to stimulate innovation and improve the adoption of new technologies by businesses.
reforms.status_evidence: The government could complement existing tax relief schemes by further increasing direct funding of R&D.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to enhance the existing funding landscape for R&D, which is crucial for fostering innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The government could complement existing tax relief schemes by further increasing direct funding of R&D.

reforms.description: The UK authorities announced an initiative to provide free basic digital skills training to all individuals, aiming to improve the skills of low-skilled workers.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing digital skills among low-skilled workers is expected to improve their employability and productivity, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The recently announced initiative by the UK authorities that entitles all individuals to free basic digital skills training.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative addresses a significant skills gap in the workforce, which is crucial for improving productivity and job quality among low-skilled individuals.
reforms.source_quote: The recently announced initiative by the UK authorities that entitles all individuals to free basic digital skills training will help to address this issue.

reforms.description: The UK government created the National Infrastructure Commission (NIC) to improve long-term infrastructure planning and investment across various sectors.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing a stable framework for infrastructure investment, the NIC aims to enhance productivity and economic growth through better planning and prioritization of projects.
reforms.status_evidence: The NIC was established to support a stable framework for infrastructure investment.
reforms.importance_rationale: The creation of the NIC represents a fundamental shift in how the UK approaches infrastructure planning, aiming for a more integrated and strategic investment framework.
reforms.source_quote: The Government has recently set up the National Infrastructure Commission (NIC) in 2015, followed by the creation of the Infrastructure and Projects Authority (IPA) in 2016.

reforms.description: The UK government has pledged to deliver superfast broadband to 95% of households by 2017 and 97% by 2019, recognizing the importance of digital connectivity for economic development.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving digital connectivity is expected to enhance productivity and economic activity across regions.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has committed to specific targets for broadband rollout.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant investment in infrastructure that supports broader economic growth and innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The government has pledged to deliver superfast broadband for 95% of households by 2017 and 97% by 2019.

reforms.description: The UK government aims to increase resources devoted to research and development (R&D) as part of its new industrial strategy, which emphasizes the importance of R&D for economic growth.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased R&D funding is expected to enhance innovation capacity and productivity across various sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has announced its commitment to increase R&D resources as part of the industrial strategy.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a fundamental shift in the government's approach to supporting innovation and economic growth through R&D investment.
reforms.source_quote: The emerging new industrial strategy has a strong focus on R&D.

reforms.description: The government aims to foster the use and effectiveness of training by introducing the Apprenticeship Levy, which is designed to improve adult skill development.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing adult skills is expected to improve productivity and innovation capacity in the workforce.
reforms.status_evidence: The government aims to foster the use and effectiveness of training by introducing the Apprenticeship Levy.
reforms.importance_rationale: The Apprenticeship Levy represents a significant step towards improving adult skills, which is crucial for enhancing productivity and addressing skill shortages in the economy.
reforms.source_quote: The government aims to foster the use and effectiveness of training by introducing the Apprenticeship Levy.

reforms.description: The National Productivity Investment Fund was announced to pledge GBP 2.3 billion on housing infrastructure to build 100,000 new homes in areas of high demand.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in housing infrastructure is expected to stimulate economic activity and improve living conditions, thereby enhancing productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The National Productivity Investment Fund pledges GBP 2.3 billion on housing infrastructure.
reforms.importance_rationale: The fund represents a significant investment in housing infrastructure, which is critical for addressing housing shortages and supporting economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The National Productivity Investment Fund pledges GBP 2.3 billion on housing infrastructure to build 100,000 new homes in areas of high demand.

reforms.description: The UK government is increasing direct and indirect support for private and public R&D, as well as promoting collaboration between businesses and universities to enhance applied innovations and their diffusion.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing R&D support is expected to boost innovation, productivity, and economic growth in lagging regions.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has committed to increasing support for R&D and collaboration.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant enhancement of existing support mechanisms aimed at fostering innovation in underperforming regions.
reforms.source_quote: Continue to increase direct and indirect support for private and public R&D, and for the collaboration between businesses and universities to promote applied innovations and their diffusion.

reforms.description: The UK government is promoting continued decentralisation by concluding deals with all city-regions, allowing local authorities to retain more revenues from locally levied property taxes.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Decentralisation is expected to enhance local governance and economic development, leading to improved business environments and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has committed to concluding deals with all city-regions.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to empower local authorities, potentially leading to better tailored economic policies and innovation support.
reforms.source_quote: Continue decentralisation by concluding deals with all city-regions and allow local authorities to retain more revenues from locally levied property taxes.

reforms.description: The government has introduced changes to the apprenticeship system to stimulate the take-up of apprenticeships and improve the technical skills of both low- and higher-skilled individuals, with a target of 3 million new apprenticeships by 2020.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing the number of apprenticeships, the reform aims to enhance the skills of the workforce, thereby improving productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has announced a very ambitious target of 3 million new apprenticeships by 2020.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the apprenticeship system, aiming to address skill shortages and improve workforce productivity on a large scale.
reforms.source_quote: The government has introduced recent changes to the apprenticeship system in order to stimulate the take-up of apprenticeships and to improve the technical skills of both low- and higher-skilled individuals.

reforms.description: The UK government plans to increase spending on research and development to enhance innovation and productivity, particularly in lagging regions and cities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased R&D spending is expected to raise innovation levels, leading to higher productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has outlined plans to boost R&D spending as part of its economic strategy.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant commitment to enhancing the UK's innovation capacity, which is crucial for long-term economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The authorities should...spending on research and development to raise innovation.
